content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 5.1.1(ii) WARN — NSLocationWhenInUseUsageDescription is literally "TODO" (App/Info.plist), a placeholder never filled in, and NSCameraUsageDescription ("This app needs camera access.") is generic and doesn't tie to the document-scanning feature seen in ScannerView.swift.
Pierre: Apple reviewers reject apps with placeholder or generic purpose strings because they don't tell users why the permission is needed, and "TODO" is an obvious leftover that will be caught immediately. Also, no location usage appears anywhere in the provided code (only camera capture in ScannerView.swift), so it's unclear why location access is requested at all — this mismatch itself may trigger a rejection. Fix by writing a specific string like "SnapScan uses your camera to scan documents" and either remove the location key if unused or explain its actual purpose (e.g., geotagging scans).
